Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Bombadil: Property-based testing for web UIs by Antithesis 56 upvotes, 7 comments
  2. Can you get root with only a cigarette lighter? (2024) 114 upvotes, 20 comments
  3. POSSE – Publish on your Own Site, Syndicate Elsewhere 251 upvotes, 53 comments
  4. An Unsolicited Guide to Being a Researcher [pdf] 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Ordered dithering with arbitrary or irregular colour palettes (2023) 60 upvotes, 9 comments
  6. Dataframe 1.0.0.0 59 upvotes, 9 comments
  7. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 211 upvotes, 47 comments
  8. The gold standard of optimization: A look under the hood of RollerCoaster Tycoon 443 upvotes, 125 comments
  9. GrapheneOS will remain usable by anyone without requiring personal information 490 upvotes, 140 comments
  10. Five years of running a systems reading group at Microsoft 183 upvotes, 52 comments
  11. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 365 upvotes, 117 comments
  12. The way CTRL-C in Postgres CLI cancels queries is incredibly hack-y 92 upvotes, 24 comments
  13. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 503 upvotes, 180 comments
  14. PC Gamer recommends RSS readers in a 37mb article that just keeps downloading 693 upvotes, 326 comments
  15. The future of version control 573 upvotes, 313 comments
  16. GitHub appears to be struggling with measly three nines availability 185 upvotes, 91 comments
  17. The LCA problem revisited [pdf] 15 upvotes, 3 comments
  18. Cyberattack on vehicle breathalyzer company leaves drivers stranded in the US 9 upvotes, 1 comments
  19. Jazz CRJ9 at New York on Mar 22nd 2026, collision with fire truck on runway 43 upvotes, 19 comments
  20. Why I love NixOS 361 upvotes, 255 comments
  21. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 462 upvotes, 337 comments
  22. Migrating to the EU 407 upvotes, 324 comments
  23. Walmart: ChatGPT checkout converted 3x worse than website 180 upvotes, 141 comments
  24. Tin Can, a 'landline' for kids 202 upvotes, 163 comments
  25. Attractive students no longer receive better results as classes moved online 160 upvotes, 137 comments
  26. Show HN: The King Wen Permutation: [52, 10, 2] 33 upvotes, 21 comments
  27. You are not your job 262 upvotes, 284 comments
  28. General Motors Is Assisting with the Restoration of a Rare EV1 37 upvotes, 31 comments
  29. What young workers are doing to AI-proof themselves 176 upvotes, 271 comments
  30. GoGoGrandparent (YC S16) is hiring Back end Engineers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 10:00pm, 23 March 2026. Source here.
